# DocVQA SeqKD recipe

Stage-1 off-policy distillation for the Qwen3.5-4B CodeAct DocVQA agent:
**SeqKD** = supervised fine-tuning on ANLS-passing trajectories generated by the
27B teacher running *our* CodeAct `agent_loop` (not the docvqa-repo dspy solver —
that format is incompatible). Loss-ladder rung 1; forward-KL top-k KD comes later.

## End-to-end pipeline

```bash
# 0) Servers: 27B at :8927 (LM+VLM). 4B trains on a freed local GPU.

# 1) Collect teacher trajectories (GPU-free; uses the running 27B server).
#    Resumable; writes one JSONL line per rollout with anls + chat messages.
python docvqa/scripts/collect_trajectories.py \
    --questions data/docvqa-2026/val/train.json \
    --lm-base-url http://localhost:8927/v1  --lm-model  Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B \
    --vlm-base-url http://localhost:8927    --vlm-model Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B \
    --num-samples-per-q 4 --temperature 0.8 --concurrency 3 \
    --output outputs/teacher_rollouts/dv2026_train_n4.jsonl --resume

# 2) Filter to anls==1.0 successful trajectories -> verl SFT parquet
#    (single `messages` column; MultiTurnSFTDataset format).
python docvqa/scripts/make_sft_data.py \
    --in  outputs/teacher_rollouts/dv2026_train_n4.jsonl \
    --out data/sft/dv2026_probe.parquet \
    --max-per-question 2

# 3) Train (LoRA, 1 GPU). See run_seqkd.sh header for env knobs.
docvqa/train/run_seqkd.sh data/sft/dv2026_probe.parquet seqkd-probe 1

# 4) Evaluate the trained adapter on DocVQA-2026 val (n=8 / pass@8 / SC-8).
#    Serve base+LoRA via vLLM, then point eval at it. See "Evaluating" below.
```

## Experiments

- **`seqkd-probe`** — in-distribution learnability probe. Train on dv2026
  val-train teacher trajectories, eval on dv2026 val-heldout + a train subset.
  Purpose: prove the training setup *can* learn before spending on the real
  transfer run. If it cannot improve even in-domain, the setup is broken.
  Graded sanity ladder: (a) overfit a handful of trajectories (loss -> ~0);
  (b) train-subset score after >> before; (c) held-out score after >= before.

- **`seqkd-transfer`** — the reportable run. Train on MMLongBench-Doc teacher
  trajectories (no DocVQA-2026 leakage), eval on DocVQA-2026 val (80 Q).

## Key correctness notes

- `data.ignore_input_ids_mismatch=True` is **required**: `MultiTurnSFTDataset`
  tokenizes each turn separately and concatenates; Qwen's `<think>` chat-template
  handling makes that differ from whole-conversation tokenization. Without this
  flag the run errors or mis-aligns the loss mask.
- Loss mask is **assistant-only** (we imitate the teacher's `<think>` + ```python```
  turns and the final `SUBMIT`, not the tool observations). Validate the mask on
  a few samples before a long run.
- CodeAct trajectories are **long** (up to ~30 turns + page observations).
  `MAX_LENGTH=32768`, `truncation=error` fail loud on overflow — if that fires,
  lower `--max-per-question`, drop the longest trajectories during data prep, or
  raise `MAX_LENGTH`. Never right-truncate: it would cut the final `SUBMIT`
  (the most important supervised tokens).

## Evaluating a trained checkpoint (verified procedure)

verl saves an FSDP-sharded checkpoint. Merge it to a plain HF model, serve that,
and point eval at it. (The `--enable-lora` path is finicky here; merging is what
worked.)

```bash
# 1) Merge FSDP checkpoint -> HF model dir (writes a full merged model.safetensors).
python -m verl.model_merger merge --backend fsdp \
    --local_dir   checkpoints/docvqa-seqkd/<exp>/global_step_<N> \
    --target_dir  checkpoints/docvqa-seqkd/<exp>/merged_hf

# 2) Qwen3.5-4B is a ConditionalGeneration (VL) model -> vLLM needs the image
#    processor config, which the merge omits. Copy it from the base cache:
BASE=$(find ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--Qwen--Qwen3.5-4B/snapshots -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | head -1)
cp -L "$BASE"/preprocessor_config.json "$BASE"/video_preprocessor_config.json \
      checkpoints/docvqa-seqkd/<exp>/merged_hf/

# 3) Serve the merged model. Do NOT set --served-model-name: eval.py loads the
#    tokenizer from --student-model AND uses it as the API id, so both must be the
#    same local path.
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=<free gpu> /home/baris/repos/prime-rl/.venv/bin/vllm serve \
    checkpoints/docvqa-seqkd/<exp>/merged_hf \
    --port 8930 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.6 --dtype bfloat16 \
    --max-model-len 65536 --enforce-eager

# 4) Eval. NOTE flags are --student-base-url / --student-model (not --lm-*).
python docvqa/scripts/eval.py \
    --questions data/docvqa-2026/val/eval_subset_strat24.json \
    --student-base-url http://localhost:8930/v1 \
    --student-model checkpoints/docvqa-seqkd/<exp>/merged_hf \
    --vlm-base-url http://localhost:8927 --vlm-model Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B \
    --concurrency 8 --n 1 --temperature 0.6 --top-p 0.95 --top-k 20 \
    --output outputs/eval/<exp>_strat24.jsonl
```

### Two-tier eval protocol
- **Dev (every iteration):** `eval_subset_strat24.json` — 24 Q, 3 per category x 8
  categories (stratified, deterministic). Eval the **baseline (untrained 4B)** and
  the **trained** model on it and compare (paired, same questions). n=1 for a quick
  signal, n=2-4 for firmer. Fast.
- **Final (chosen model only):** full `questions.json` (80 Q), n=8, report
  mean±std / pass@8 / SC-8 (spec §9), then the official test.
- Eval is VLM-call-latency-bound, so raise `--concurrency` (the 27B VLM is far from
  saturated); the eval output is a JSON array. `eval.py` writes results at the end.
